title: World, Weather & Time Control
description: Learn to manipulate Minecraft worlds, control weather, and adjust time using plugins.

lessons.title: World Management Basics
lessons.content:
## Introduction
Minecraft worlds can be accessed and controlled using the Bukkit/Paper API.

### Example: Teleporting a Player to a Specific World
```java
World world = Bukkit.getWorld("world_nether");
if(world != null) {
    player.teleport(world.getSpawnLocation());
}
```

### Logic Explanation
- `Bukkit.getWorld(name)` retrieves the world object.
- Always check for null (world might not exist).
- `teleport()` moves the player safely.

### Edge Cases & Warnings
- Attempting to teleport to a null world causes errors.
- Ensure world is loaded (`Bukkit.createWorld()` if needed).

quiz.question: What does `Bukkit.getWorld("world_nether")` return if the world does not exist?
Which method moves a player to a location?

lessons.title: Changing Weather
lessons.content:
## Introduction
You can control weather conditions in a world programmatically.

### Example: Clear Weather Command
```java
World world = player.getWorld();
world.setStorm(false); // stop rain
world.setThundering(false); // stop thunder
world.setWeatherDuration(6000); // duration in ticks
```

### Logic Explanation
- `setStorm(false)` stops rain.
- `setThundering(false)` stops thunder.
- Weather duration is in ticks (20 ticks = 1 second).

### Edge Cases & Warnings
- Players might see delayed weather changes due to client sync.
- Avoid repeatedly changing weather every tick (performance issues).

quiz.question: How many ticks are in one second in Minecraft?

lessons.title: Setting World Time
lessons.content:
## Introduction
You can manipulate the time in a world to create day/night cycles or custom events.

### Example: Set World to Noon
```java
World world = player.getWorld();
world.setTime(6000); // 0 = sunrise, 6000 = noon, 12000 = sunset, 18000 = midnight
```

### Logic Explanation
- Time is in ticks, ranging 0-23999.
- Use `world.getTime()` to read current time.
- Can combine with scheduled tasks for dynamic day/night cycles.

### Edge Cases & Warnings
- Rapidly changing time may look glitchy to players.
- Be careful on servers with multiple worlds, adjust the correct world only.

quiz.question: Which method reads the current world time?
Rapidly changing time every tick can cause?
